task_completion.description: Verify that 'sales_data.csv' has been imported into Power BI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Data' or 'Fields' pane on the right side of the Power BI interface shows the 'sales_data' table with its respective columns (e.g., Product_ID, Quantity_Sold, Sales_Amount).

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of a scatter chart with Product_ID, Sum of Quantity_Sold, and Sum of Sales_Amount.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A scatter chart is visible on the report canvas. In the Visualizations pane, 'Product_ID' is in the Details/Values well, 'Quantity_Sold' (as Sum) is in the X-axis well, and 'Sales_Amount' (as Sum) is in the Y-axis well.

task_completion.description: Verify that exactly 3 clusters were generated using the scatter chart.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The scatter chart displays data points grouped into exactly 3 distinct colors/clusters. A new cluster field should be visible in the legend or Data pane.

task_completion.description: Verify the new cluster field is renamed to 'Product_Segment'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Data'/'Fields' pane or the scatter chart's Legend well shows the cluster field named exactly 'Product_Segment'.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of a table visual displaying Product_Segment, count of Product_ID, and average Sales_Amount.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A table visual is present on the canvas containing three columns: 'Product_Segment', 'Count of Product_ID' (or similar count aggregation), and 'Average of Sales_Amount'.

task_completion.description: Verify the final report is saved as 'Product_Segmentation.pbix' to the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Power BI window title bar displays 'Product_Segmentation - Power BI Desktop', or a file explorer window shows 'Product_Segmentation.pbix' located on the Desktop.

integrity.description: The agent used the Power BI Desktop GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the Power BI interface, dragging fields, and using the ribbon, rather than running terminal commands or scripts to generate the file.

integrity.description: The agent used Power BI's built-in clustering feature rather than manually grouping data or faking the visual.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Automatically find clusters' dialog or clustering process is visible in the trajectory, and the resulting field has the characteristic cluster icon in the Data pane.

integrity.description: The agent built the report from scratch rather than copying a pre-made file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows the step-by-step creation of the visuals and the clustering process on a blank canvas.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output is a Power BI report named 'Product_Segmentation.pbix'. The report must contain exactly 3 clusters generated from a scatter chart. The scatter chart must use Product_ID for details, Sum of Quantity_Sold for the X-axis, and Sum of Sales_Amount for the Y-axis. The generated cluster field must be renamed to 'Product_Segment'. A table visual must also be present, displaying 'Product_Segment', count of Product_ID, and average Sales_Amount.
